items.title: Try the viewer with sample data
items.body: You can now check out the viewer before signing in. There's a button on the home page that loads two weeks of fake sessions so you can see what your own data would look like.

items.title: Stays connected through the night
items.body: The app is much better at recovering when the strap briefly drops out, like when you roll over in your sleep. Long sessions also use less battery and data.

items.title: Viewer fixes
items.body: The chills slider in the viewer actually does something now — drag it to see more or fewer events. Sundays in the week view open properly. The autonomic summary card no longer always says Neutral when one zone clearly dominated. HR shifts are easier to spot too — they're purple now instead of looking the same blue as chills.

items.title: Works alongside Sleep As Android
items.body: Aorti can now share your strap with sleep-tracking apps at the same time. You don't have to disconnect one to use the other.

items.title: Save CSV anywhere, broadcast on/off, offline mode
items.body: Tapping Export CSV now opens a save dialog so the file goes wherever you want. The relay-broadcast switch actually turns broadcasting on and off. You can also record a private session without broadcasting at all.
